name: Gyeongsangnam-do (South Korea)

colors.symbolism: purity, peace and the bright future of the province
the southern sea and the long coastline of the province

description: A white field charged at the centre with a stylised emblem in blue, green and red, evoking an abstract sun, mountains and waves, with the wordmark 'GYEONGNAM' below.
history: South Gyeongsang Province (Gyeongsangnam-do, also known as Gyeongnam), occupying the south-eastern corner of the Korean peninsula and home to the country's principal port (Busan, now a separate Metropolitan City) and shipbuilding industry, has used several provincial flags. The current design dates from a redesign in the 2010s, with the abstract emblem representing the southern sea, the surrounding mountains and the rising sun.

features.symbolism: abstract motif of waves, mountains and sun, representing the southern sea, the surrounding mountains, and the dynamic, industrial character of the province
